intro: Grastidium truncicola, commonly known as the blue bush or blue mat-rush, is a small, mat-forming plant species native to Western Australia. The health benefits of Grastidium truncicola include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoid glycosides, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Grastidium truncicola include teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used to treat respiratory conditions and as a natural expectorant.
